programmes.show_description: Willys Jeep: Tucked away in the showroom is a WW2-era Willys Jeep. Its original condition means there might be a bunfight for a military vehicle that's ripe for restoration.
Austin Healey: Up for auction this week is a 1980 Ford Escort, a hand-built 1946 Bedford 12 electric car, and a beautiful, red 1957 Austin Healey.

programmes.show_name: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programmes.show_description: Cat Among the Pigeons: Lucy Worsley explores Agatha Christie's haunted, unconventional early life to discover the origins of her talent for murder.
Shopping from Home.
Avonmouth to Six Bells: Michael Portillo crosses the Severn to begin a trip along the south coast of Wales.
Barry Island to Pyle: Michael Portillo continues his south Wales coastal railway journey in Barry Island.
Port Talbot to Pembrey Burry Port: Michael Portillo's coastal rail journey along the south coast of Wales reaches the town of Port Talbot.
Carmarthen to Pembroke: Michael Portillo travels the rural coastline of Carmarthenshire and Pembrokeshire in south Wales.
Triumph TR4A: Derek collects a beautifully restored and carefully modified 1936 Wolseley 14/56. Meanwhile, a Batman-inspired Chrysler Crossfire is dropped off at the Thornton le Dale garage.
Vauxhall Cresta: Paul's doing the pick-ups in Lancashire where a rare 1960 Vauxhall Cresta PA Friary estate in dusky pink awaits.
Dambuster Factory: How the Lancaster factory, one of the biggest buildings in Europe at the time, helped to create a truly war-winning weapon.
Night of the Long Knives: The infamous Night of the Long Knives in late June and early July of 1934, when Adolf Hitler ordered a series of political executions on those seen as a threat.
Buckfast 1: Fiona Bruce presents from Buckfast Abbey in Devon, where experts examine an 18th-century tankard, an iron chest and a piece of shrapnel from the battleship Bismarck.
Aerospace 1: Fiona Bruce presents from the Concorde Hangar at the Aerospace Bristol museum, where the most surprising item brought in is a very valuable statue of Buddha.

Motorised Bicycles/Nissan Sunny: Derek's grandchildren, Jack and Amy, head to the North East of England to pick up some charming old bikes. Derek helps a collector downsize and discovers a rare Chrysl
Austin 7/Austin Ruby/Ferrari Reps: Jack's drawn the short straw again as he attempts to shift a couple of 1930's Austin Sevens that have been stuck in the same spot for half a century.
Dambuster Factory: How the Lancaster factory, one of the biggest buildings in Europe at the time, helped to create a truly war-winning weapon.
Night of the Long Knives: The infamous Night of the Long Knives in late June and early July of 1934, when Adolf Hitler ordered a series of political executions on those seen as a threat.
Buckfast 1: Fiona Bruce presents from Buckfast Abbey in Devon, where experts examine an 18th-century tankard, an iron chest and a piece of shrapnel from the battleship Bismarck.
In the third leg of his journey, Simon Reeve is in the United States and travelling down the length of California to explore the land of the giant redwoods and the West Coast.
Rascal / Triumph TR6: The team head for restoration in their Bedford Rascal Campervan. They also purchase a true British classic - a TR6 - which is almost too good to touch!
Rolls Royce Phantom/VW Bettle: Derek's charmed by a 1929 Rolls Royce Phantom with an unusual history, involving a WW1 hero and a tale of fraud.

Austin 7/Austin Ruby/Ferrari Reps: Jack's drawn the short straw again as he attempts to shift a couple of 1930's Austin Sevens that have been stuck in the same spot for half a century.

Vauxhall Cresta: Paul's doing the pick-ups in Lancashire where a rare 1960 Vauxhall Cresta PA Friary estate in dusky pink awaits.

programmes.show_name: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programmes.show_description: Destination Unknown: Lucy Worsley examines Agatha's mysterious disappearance in December 1926 and reveals the influence this episode had on her writing.
Shopping from Home.
Milford Haven to Fishguard: Michael Portillo reaches the most westerly station in Wales, on the final leg of his rail journey along its southern coastline.
Douglas to Port Erin: Michael Portillo returns to travel by train to more places around the British Isles, beginning by heading to the Isle of Man - a paradise for rail lovers.
Liverpool to Blackpool: Michael Portillo journeys to Liverpool's historic waterfront, where he visits the International Slavery Museum, before heading north towards Blackpool.
Morecambe to Grange-over-Sands: Michael Portillo continues his journey across the vast and beautiful Morecambe Bay, where he joins one of the town's last shrimpers.
Daf 66 Marathon Coupe: A vivid Dutch DAF 66 - in daffodil yellow - lands at the North Yorkshire showroom.
Jaguar 2+2 E Type: Paul's on a big day out collecting a 1971 Jaguar E-Type and a 1933 Morgan 3-wheeler Supersport but first there's a flock of 300 chickens to negotiate before he can load up.
Colt & Kalashnikov: Long before Henry Ford, Samuel Colt is the true father of mass production. Ironically, it was the inadequacy of Soviet factory production which made the AK47 so effective.
Barbarossa: Hitler now faces fellow ruthless dictator Josef Stalin and tries to deceive him with a plan to invade Britain while preparing an attack on the Soviet Union.
Piece Hall 1: Fiona Bruce presents from Piece Hall in Halifax, where items featured include a huge sapphire which the owner is convinced is a fake and a Chinese imperial robe.
Wrest Park 1: Fiona Bruce presents from Wrest Park, Bedfordshire, where finds include a diamond and ruby pendant, a rare snuff container, and competing pieces of Daum glassware.

Bentley/Riley Kestrel/Cosworth: Derek heads to North Wales to collect an Austin 7, and a gem of a Bentley, a British classic with coachwork built by top notch Parisian outfit Kellner who specialised i
BSA Bikes/Athena/VW Beetle: Derek heads to Doncaster to collect four vintage BSAs motorbikes including one said to be the rarest BSA of all.

Simon Reeve travels through Texas and Mexico and joins the US Border Patrol - who pick up migrants who have illegally crossed the Rio Grande river in search of a new life - on the way.
Dauphine / Omega: A super low mileage Renault Dauphine proves irresistible to the Resto team... But its 1959 mechanics ensure some hefty bills.
Daimler Sovereign Coupe: Irv and the team update a 1977 Daimler Sovereign Coupe giving it a slick, modern makeover. However, a disaster in the paint booth puts the project in peril.
